programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between the Citizens and Hornets since the FA Cup final in May, when Pep Guardiola's men ran riot in a thumping 6-0 victory at Wembley Stadium. That was City's 11th successive victory over Watford, a record stretching back to April 2007. Plus, action from Everton v Sheffield United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at the King Power Stadium, Newcastle United v Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park, and Southampton v Bournemouth, which took place on Friday night at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: Countryfile

Matt Baker and Helen Skelton present proceedings from Bowhill House, as the best youngsters and senior shepherds from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland are teamed up in a bid to win the trophy for their country(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, on the east coast of Scotland. She explores the fascinating story behind the landmark museum and the treasures it houses. Among the discoveries are a valuable bowl found at a car-boot sale for just £3, a set of rare SAS medals and a Vivienne Westwood jacket(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: News of Tommy's activities has reached Winston Churchill. At a family meeting, Tommy lays out his plans for Mosley, which will take place at a rally Sir Oswald is leading. Has he underestimated his opponent? Tommy is surprised by another strategy coming from an unexpected corner, and he also has his own secrets to reveal. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Sam Claflin, Paul Anderson, Helen McCrory and Cosmo Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool at Stamford Bridge, as the Blues looked to avenge last season's 2-1 defeat here. Plus, Crystal Palace v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Selhurst Park, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium, Arsenal v Aston Villa at Emirates Stadium, and a round-up of yesterday's goals(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Burnley defender James Tarkowski, Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip, IPC World Championships long jump champion Stef Reid and Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Dream House (2011)
programme.desc: A man moves to a small town with his wife and two daughters, but receives the startling news that a woman and her children were murdered in his new home five years previously. He begins seeing things which make him suspect the house is haunted, and that there may be a connection between the ghosts and his own past. Horror, starring Daniel Craig, Naomi Watts and Rachel Weisz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: The week starts in London as Michelle Ackerley joins Metropolitan Police officers at their Hendon base, where the force trains officers in everything from forensics to self-defence(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Dartford, Kent, Stoke and Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers are on the trail of a conman, and crack down on landlords putting tenants' lives at risk, with filmed evidence from one man showing smoke and fire damage to his room(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A lorry driver trapped for hours in his vehicle after swerving to avoid a deer, and a family searching for their lost dog on the beach call for help when dad gets stuck in the mud(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, as Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help the teams look for items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara and Emma sign up for romance. Becky tells Ayesha she is really falling for Daniel, Karen gives an old school friend some home truths, and Sid has a hairy moment with a patient(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Hook, Hampshire to help young couple Cat and Joel. The newlyweds dream of a family garden for the children that they plan to have - a space full of grass and wildlife friendly flowers. However, all they have now is a concrete garden made up of nothing but paving stones(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the country lanes of Northern Ireland in their Rolls-Royce. Angus has designs on a piece of Victorian furniture while Roo hits the dance floor(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball returns with the weekday fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing extravaganza, and is joined by her new co-host Rylan Clark-Neal to run down the latest gossip(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out South
programme.desc: A report on parents going to great lengths to obtain a drug for treating children with cystic fibrosis, and the story of the man behind the Kenwood Chef(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel realises she can use her discovery to take revenge on the Mitchells, so she enlists Lisa's help to get hold of Keanu's phone and set a trap for Sharon. The Taylors gather to celebrate Keegan's birthday, but the celebrations are ruined by tension between Gray and Mitch, while Tiffany's attempts to get Keegan on his own seem fruitless. Louise is less than impressed to see Ben is well and truly back in the Mitchell fold, and Ted and Patrick are nervous about meeting Sheree's mum, Wanda(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Footballer-turned-property expert Dion Dublin, comedians Bob Mortimer and Lucy Porter, and the lovely Debbie McGee join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: EastEnders stalwart Adam Woodyatt, Geordie Shore's Vicky Pattison, former boxing promoter Kellie Maloney, weather presenter Tomasz Schafernaker and DJ and lawyer Judge Jules take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. Armed with just a pestle and mortar, the celebs' first task is to create a curry of their own design using a larder of fresh spices, chilies and herbs and a range of meat, fish and vegetables. They must then spend time in a professional restaurant kitchen, cooking for paying customers in a busy lunchtime service(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: Family loyalty is vital in rural communities and Kerry's life-long ambition has been to work with her father, Martin. She is overjoyed when he asks her to help him store some wares in a lock-up garage. Meanwhile, a job at the local bowls club gives Kurtan a new sense of purpose(n)

programme.title: FIFA Football Awards 2019
programme.desc: Highlights from the Teatro alla Scala in Milan, Italy, as the finest players and coaches from both the men's and women's games were rewarded for their performances over the last year. Virgil van Dijk, Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo were the nominees for the prestigious Best FIFA Men's Player award claimed by Luca Modric in 2018(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The first in a two-part documentary drawing on an interview with former prime minister David Cameron to explore his leadership, the events that led to the EU referendum and its impact on the political landscape - not to mention his own career. The first film examines how the prime minister went from riding high after the successful 2015 election campaign to defeat in the referendum. Including contributions from Nick Clegg, Iain Duncan Smith, Michael Gove, George Osborne, Ann Widdecombe and Michael Heseltine(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present a report on counter-terrorism measures in a busy working airport. Plus, more calls for information on unsolved crimes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of a Welsh chapel and terraced houses in Newcastle-under-Lyme and Offerton as they are sold at auction(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate the tragic death of a seven-year-old girl when the bouncy castle she was playing on was swept away by a gust of wind. They also enlist the help of soccer superstar Wayne Rooney as they chase down a couple making a fortune out of fake football memorabilia(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles tells the story of two young students mowed down in a hit and run and a lone climber on Snowdon takes a tumble in a spot where the rescue helicopter cannot reach him(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At Newark International Antiques and Collectors Fair in Nottinghamshire, experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper help two teams pick up items to sell for profit at auction. Presented by Christina Trevanion(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel makes a decision about the future, and Ruhma finds herself in a bizarre situation with a pizza delivery man, a porter and a woman who does not want a father for her baby(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team helps a woman living in Salford to recreate the traditional German garden her family had when she was a child, with space divided up for fruit, vegetables and lawn(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth traverse south-west Wales, where they learn about traditional boats and sheep farming before attending an auction in Carmarthen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal presents more backstage gossip, joined on the sofa by some of this series' dancers. Plus, the weekend's performances are examined in Choreography Corner(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the pressure from Mel rising, Sharon suggests going for lunch with Keanu and Louise to talk but is frustrated when Phil invites himself along. Ben tries to impress Jason, one of Phil's business associates, by telling him he can get him a good deal on a fancy car. Ted and Wanda's second date ends in disaster, Rainie loses control after Ian confronts her about her drug-taking and Gray tells Chantelle he's going to see a therapist about his behaviour(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Evan shows no sign of giving up and pursues Chloe to a cottage in the countryside, where his obsession with her takes a violent turn that leaves her in fear for her life. Cameron receives bad news that forces him to abandon his efforts to help Ric, who has finally been forced to acknowledge his deteriorating health(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Rachel finally comes face to face with Shaun, who offers up the location of the CIA safe house before managing to evade arrest once more. The fugitive soldier flees into an encounter with two mysterious strangers, who offer to provide him with the answers he is looking for. Rachel pays Napier a visit, and the meeting leaves her more convinced than ever that the CCTV network has been compromised(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Documentary following six junior doctors beginning new placements at Salford Royal hospital. A doctor assigned to the geriatrics ward has to work on his bedside manner, while a colleague in the colorectal ward has her knowledge of bowel surgery put to the test and is given the difficult task of delivering some bad news to a patient(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Jacksonville Jaguars v Tennessee Titans at TIAA Bank Field(n)

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: During March and April, the horticulturist clears the detritus left behind by winter, as celandines, violets and primroses begin to bloom. She plants the first seeds of the year and is joined by a local gardener to prune the box hedge, but their work is interrupted by a nesting sparrow(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letters X, Y and Z, concluding her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don assesses the progress, successes and failures of the paradise garden he planted at the start of the year. He also sets about sowing a meadow of wildflowers in the orchard and gives advice on what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Frances Tophill travels to Glasgow to meet a gardener whose hands-off approach to his garden has reaped rewards for wildlife, Adam Frost visits a garden in Wales where a potentially unpromising and rocky site has been transformed, while Mark Lane explores Nymans in West Sussex to find out how the garden is managed with sustainability at its heart(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets a larger-than-life character in Dorset - The Cerne Abbas Giant. It's 100 years since the Giant was gifted to the National Trust, and he needs to be smartened up, so Ellie joins a group of volunteers to re-chalk the figure, hoping to restore it to its former glory. Steve Brown visits the world's only managed flock of Mute swans at Abbotsbury Swannery, while Adam Henson meets the Welsh and Scottish contenders hoping to lift this year's One Man and His Dog trophy(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1938)
programme.desc: A traveller strikes up a conversation with a scatterbrained elderly lady when their train is delayed, but after the journey resumes, her new friend mysteriously vanishes. Almost everyone else on board pleads ignorance about the matter, prompting her and a fellow passenger to investigate the intriguing occurrence. Alfred Hitchcock's mystery, starring Margaret Lockwood, Michael Redgrave, May Whitty, Paul Lukas and Cecil Parker(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the television appearances made over the years by pioneering Hollywood film director Alfred Hitchcock, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the inaugural mixed time-trial relay, which features a 28km circuit starting and finishing in Harrogate, with three male and female riders from each participating country battling it out for the gold. Plus, highlights of Saturday's para-cycling race(n)

programme.title: Animals in Love
programme.desc: In the second of two programmes about relationships in the wild, Liz Bonnin explores the ways in which animals get together. She observes a form of extreme flirting in a troop of capuchin monkeys in South America, which sees the females go to extraordinary lengths to attract the alpha male, reveals how African hyenas find it tough getting a girl and how an orang-utan in America chooses its partner in a very 21st-century way. For other animals, it turns out smell is a deal-breaker, while for others, it's all about having the best moves on the dance floor(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A lifeboat crew in Dorset faces the challenge of getting urgent medical aid to a rock climber, who has fallen more than 40 feet and landed on an inaccessible rocky ledge. In Cornwall, a paraglider misjudges his launch and ends up crashing into the sea, while the Redcar crew search for a man who has drifted over a mile offshore in a small toy dinghy(n)

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day at Wentworth, where Francesco Molinari was victorious last year. The Italian started the day with a share of the lead alongside Rory McIlroy, but his final four-under-par round of 68 helped him to finish two strokes clear of the Northern Irishman and claim a first prize of nearly 1million euros. With commentary from Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In the wake of the disruption at Gatwick last December, high-risk specialist Aldo Kane investigates the scale of the threat that drones pose to UK airspace and airports - from rogue hobbyists to terrorist attacks. He explores what technology is out there that governments and the aviation industry can turn to keep the skies safe(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A Yorkshire shepherd and his business partner hope the Dragons will flock to invest in their field-to-fork lamb delivery boxes. An inclusive fashion designer brings a little street style to the Den and a confectionery pioneer hopes to bag a sweet Dragon deal with his low sugar, dairy-free chocolate(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: The presenter meets bounty hunters in the US, who are hired to track down fugitives who have skipped bail on behalf of the bondsman. Most of the American states grant these freelance operatives tremendous legal power and freedom to act. However, they receive little training, and almost anyone can go into business as a bounty hunter. The programme also explores the workings of the commercial bail system(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox helps more people living and working in remote rural areas to find love. The first episode introduces Grace, a sheep farmer from Wales, and Martin, a dairy farmer from Yorkshire. Grace faces inheriting her father's business when he retires, so is looking for a partner who will be willing to share the responsibility. Martin has not had a relationship in more than a decade, so leaves behind his Doncaster roots to attend a speed dating event in London attended by women from across the UK who have written to the show(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: The young volunteers begin their third week at the Royal Derby Hospital and Senior Nurse Karen gives them more responsibilities, as she sends four volunteers to two new wards. One of the youngest volunteers on the programme is Fin, who, at 19 years old, wasn't sensitive enough to the needs on the Cancer Ward and was asked to leave. Fin and Michael are moved to the Elderly Care Ward, where they are tasked with providing care for 101-year-old patient John, a wartime Spitfire pilot(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Southampton, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week. The panellists are Minister for Women Victoria Atkins, Labour peer Charles Falconer, Liberal Democrat deputy leader Ed Davey, Novara Media contributing editor Ash Sarkar and the associate editor of the Daily Telegraph, Camilla Tominey(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric fears for his health, leading him to withdraw just as his granddaughter goes into premature labour. Cameron steps in to take charge, and forces Ric to face up to his fears. Essie's false claim to be Isla's adoptive mother stands to be exposed when the girl requires emergency surgery, and Chloe throws herself back into work in the hope of moving on from Evan - but is gripped by a paranoid belief that someone is spying on her(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton tour Izzie's native Derbyshire on the final leg, where she tempts the gourmand with some local delicacies. There's a blast from the past as James is carried away by the news from the 1780s while Izzie follows in the tracks of Winston Churchill. Then, it's onwards to auction in Lincoln(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the east coast of Scotland, volunteers from Dunbar race to save three children trapped in a cave by rising tides, while in the west, the Tobermory crew comes to the aid of a yacht caught in a storm and being forced onto the rocks. In England, the Blyth crew searches for an 80-year-old man who has gone missing after leaving home to look for lobsters on the coast(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn presents live coverage of the Labour Party autumn conference in Brighton, including Shadow Chancellor John McDonnell's speech(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Families from Milton Keynes and Staffordshire test drive a new way of life as they swap homes with each other. Presented by Gyles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the third and fourth days of the prestigious European Tour event from Wentworth. With commentary by Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A Tooting man discovers an ancestor was one of Britain's early tobacco merchants. While on the case of a retired Post Office worker, a visit to his flat leads to a discovery(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where Christina Trevanion and David Fletcher assess items including a large collection of boxed Corgi toys and some rare watches. Meanwhile, Paul Martin discovers the dramatic political life of one of Sandon's earlier owners, who was a member of William Pitt's cabinet in tumultuous times(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from East Yorkshire to Caernarfon, beginning by travelling from the city of Hull to the North Yorkshire market town of Malton(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenter Michelle Ackerley and astrologer Russell Grant team up with experts Roo Irvine and Phil Serrell for an antiques journey on one of the hottest days of the summer. The two teams traverse the Home Counties in search of antiques to sell at auction. Russell is drawn to all things sparkly and at the modern end of the market, while Phil struggles to convince Michelle to buy classic pieces of furniture(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as electropop fans the Electrophiles take on family team the Forrests. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Ground Force, Executive, Marine and Air Force(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The ninth first-round match sees four students from Goldsmiths, University of London pit their wits against a team from the University of Southampton in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The $50m Art Swindle
programme.desc: Documentary telling the remarkable story of a charlatan art dealer who swindled more than $50million from the art establishment before going on the run. Michel Cohen, a popular and charming New York art dealer originally from France, was a high-school drop-out from a poor background who went on to become a rich and successful art dealer. Throughout the 1990s, he sold paintings by artists such as Picasso, Monet and Chagall to America's wealthiest elite. Cohen was living the high life until he began trading recklessly in the stock market and ran up considerable debts. In an attempt to recoup his losses, he swindled private collectors, auction houses and other art dealers out of more than $50million. When his crimes were discovered, he fled the US with his wife and two children and went on the run. In 2003, he was found by Interpol in Brazil and was imprisoned in Rio de Janeiro, but seven months later while awaiting extradition to the US, he escaped from prison and vanished off the face o(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Today at Conference
programme.desc: Jo Coburn presents highlights from the Labour Party autumn conference in Brighton(n)

programme.title: 1944: Should We Bomb Auschwitz?
programme.desc: In 1944 two Jewish prisoners escaped from Auschwitz. After they told the world of the horror of the Holocaust, it raised one of the greatest moral questions of the 20th century - should the Allies bomb the camp? This documentary tells the whole of the story, combining dramatic reconstructions of the arguments that took place at the highest levels with first-hand testimony from survivors to explore a moral dilemma that still resonates today - how to act in the face of genocide(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The five remaining contestants take part in a commercial design challenge to transform holiday lodges in the Lincolnshire woods. Split into a team of two and a team of three, the competitors embark on the biggest project of the series so far, designing a bedroom each, along with a shared kitchen-diner and an outdoor area. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets a larger-than-life character in Dorset - The Cerne Abbas Giant. It's 100 years since the Giant was gifted to the National Trust, and he needs to be smartened up, so Ellie joins a group of volunteers to re-chalk the figure, hoping to restore it to its former glory. Steve Brown visits the world's only managed flock of Mute swans at Abbotsbury Swannery, while Adam Henson meets the Welsh and Scottish contenders hoping to lift this year's One Man and His Dog trophy(n)

The newlyweds dream of a family garden for the children that they plan to have - a space full of grass and wildlife friendly flowers. However, all they have now is a concrete garden made up of nothing but paving stones(n)

programme.title: Transplant Games
programme.desc: Alan Shearer tells the story of athletes whose lives were transformed by transplant surgery(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales, where they talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table - in a local pub. In the evening, Paul organises some entertainment, while Bob struggles to cook a heart healthy dinner to round off their trip(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Gaby Roslin presents a lifeline appeal on behalf of WellChild - a charity helping to get seriously ill children and young people out of hospital and home to their families(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team goes island-hopping around the Western Isles and out to Shetland. Nick Crane hunts for gannets on Eriskay, and Neil Oliver hears the tragic story of a 1918 shipwreck off the Isle of Lewis in which more than 200 servicemen returning from the First World War were drowned. Plus, Miranda Krestovnikoff searches for otters which struggle to survive on the wild coast, and Tessa Dunlop investigates how the epic voyage of HMS Challenger in the 19th century first revealed the secrets of life in the depths of the world's oceans(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Two artistic pairs of swappers from South Glamorgan and Gloucestershire spend time in one another's homes and reveal what they have learned from the experience(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the women's individual time-trial, as the riders take on a 30km course from Ripon to Harrogate, which features a flat first 12km of the race and an undulating second half. The Netherlands dominated this event last year, with Annemiek van Vleuten leading home a Dutch one-two-three(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Powderham Castle in Devon, antiques experts Will Axon and Mark Stacey find several treasures to take off to auction, including a shotgun case that belonged to a First World War hero and a pristine collection of Dinky toys. Plus, Paul Martin delves into the past of the castle, which has been home to the same family for more than 600 years, and uncovers a scandal that changed the course of that family's history(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his Edwardian Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo conducts important research in an historic tea room, built by an Edwardian immigrant to the city of York. In Leeds he discovers the city's textile heritage, and also investigates Bradford's musical heritage(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which famous faces compete in the cross-country bargain-hunting challenge. TV presenters Denise Van Outen and Kelly Brook set out on a trip around south-east England in classic cars, receiving expert guidance from Charles Hanson and Tim Medhurst as they visit antique shops and emporiums to find items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A crew of RNLI volunteers in Newquay is called out to a shipwreck off the Cornish coast, where a diver who has lost his mouthpiece needs rescuing from 30 metres underwater. A yacht in the Dover Strait starts to sink - and may collide with a cargo ship before going under - and a Dorset crew search for a windsurfer who has got caught in a storm(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Katy picks her four favourite letter writers to meet face-to-face at a speed dating event in London, including 74-year-old former professional footballer Carl, and painter and decorator John. Meanwhile, it's decision time for dairy farmers Martin and Grace, who have each invited three potential suitors back to their farms. Both are looking for a partner that will fall in love with not only them, but also their rural lifestyle and are hoping sparks will fly with at least one of their guests during their weekend stay(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: As Will grapples with whether to tell his girlfriend about his recent indiscretion - but he has no such dilemma when in Chambers, as Liam has already told everyone. However, his newfound notoriety does throw up a juicy opportunity from Miles, who offers him the case that everyone wants. Meanwhile, Caroline fears her prospects of becoming a QC are dubious, and wonders how much to let the jury know about a particularly unappealing client. Legal sitcom, starring Will Sharpe and Katherine Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Today at Conference
programme.desc: Jo Coburn presents highlights from the Labour Party autumn conference in Brighton(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the third round of fixtures, including Kansas City Chiefs v Baltimore Ravens, San Francisco 49ers v Pittsburgh Steelers, and Seattle Seahawks v New Orleans Saints(n)

programme.title: The Last Night of the Proms from Around the UK
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown and Josie d'Arby introduce a whistle stop tour around the UK, as the nations join in the Last Night of the Proms celebrations. Josie is at Swansea's Singleton Park with 80s pop sensations ABC, West End star Sophie Evans, 2019 BBC Cardiff Singer of the World Andrei Kymach, and BBC Young Jazz Musician 2018 winner, saxophonist Xhosa Cole. At Glasgow Green, Barbara Dickson entertains the crowds alongside Scottish soprano Eleanor Dennis and violinist Stephanie Childress, while Susan Boyle headlines at Titanic Slipways, Belfast, and alongside her in the line-up are Australian tenor Mark Vincent and award-winning violinist Ziyu He. At London's Hyde Park, YolanDa Brown is on hand to capture a line-up that includes Chrissie Hynde, Jack Savoretti and Barry Manilow(n)

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Documentary following artist Antony Gormley as he works in his King's Cross studio on sculptures known as Tanker Field - a group of giant steel figures to be exhibited at the Paul Klee museum in Bern, Switzerland. The film offers an intimate insight into the popular sculptor and the development of the pieces(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Documentary that exposes a darker, less well-known side of film director Ingmar Bergman. Focusing on 1957, a landmark year in which Bergman directed two films and four plays, Jane Magnusson explores not only the director's filmography but also his, at times, complex and turbulent personal life(n)

programme.title: The Seventh Seal (1957)
programme.desc: A disillusioned knight returns home from the Crusades to find his home ravaged by the Black Death. He is soon confronted by the Grim Reaper, who tells him it is his time. In order to prolong his life, he challenges the spectral figure to a game of chess, and as they play, he reflects on life, death and the existence of God. Ingmar Bergman's drama, starring Max von Sydow and Bengt Ekerot. In Swedish and Latin(n)

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: Derek Jacobi goes in search of the truth about 18th-century actor David Garrick, who reinvented acting for the modern era(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The gripping drama is halfway through and the twists and turns come thick and fast as Tommy shares some important family news with his brothers, and outlines his plans for the most audacious criminal act the gang has ever undertaken. He also finds himself playing a dangerous game with Tatiana as they attempt to acquire information from each another. Meanwhile, a guilty Polly makes an ill-advised confession - with terrible consequences for her nephew. Cillian Murphy stars, with Helen McCrory, Paddy Considine and Gaite Jansen(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Russians test the Shelbys and their gang, and Tommy begins to realise he may have underestimated his new business partners, and has now been seriously outmanoeuvred. As aristocrats exiled by the Soviet Revolution, the Petrovnas have absolutely nothing to lose, and beneath their civilised veneer lies a fierce determination to regain what they have lost. However, the Birmingham mobster still has an ace up his sleeve, in the form of an old enemy now working as an ally. The question is, can he control him? Tom Hardy returns as menacing mobster Alfie Solomons, with Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory, Annabelle Wallis and Paul Anderson(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The third run of Steven Knight's period drama reaches its final episode, and yet again Cillian Murphy as Thomas Shelby finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation. Has his criminal life finally caught up with the Birmingham-based mobster? Tommy prepares to commit the most audacious crime of his career, only to receive an unexpected blow that puts him under unbearable pressure. In a nerve-shredding race against time, Tommy is forced to confront hard truths about himself and his family that could change the Peaky Blinders for ever. As he faces his worst fears he needs help more than ever - but is increasingly uncertain about who he can trust(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: Nesting season is in full swing as spring arrives and while hundreds of dotterel are resting in the Black Mountains during their journey from Africa to breeding sites in Scotland, peregrines are already nesting in an old quarry in the Central Beacons. Iolo Williams also explores the most crooked church in Britain and an old gunpowder works and finds that a 300-year-old stone wall reveals the history of this landscape(n)

programme.title: Nelson's Caribbean Hell-Hole: An Eighteenth Century Navy Graveyard Uncovered
programme.desc: After the discovery of human bones on a beach in Antigua, historian Sam Willis investigates one of the darkest chapters of Britain's imperial past. As archaeologists excavate a mass grave of British soldiers, he explores the island's ruins and discovers how the sugar islands of the Caribbean were rife with sun, sea, war, tropical diseases and poisoned rum(n)

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a journey from Lucknow in Uttar Pradesh to Kolkata in West Bengal, Michael Portillo charts a course through India's history from the days of The East India Company to the dawn of independence. In Lucknow, he tastes the famous kebabs, stops off at Bodh Gaya, where he meditates on its Buddhist heritage, and In Kolkata, he glimpses colonial Calcutta in the grand Great Eastern Hotel(n)

programme.title: Tornado: The 100mph Steam Engine
programme.desc: Cameras following the fortunes of new A1 class steam engine Tornado as it tries to achieve 100mph on the main line, a secret attempt made in the middle of the night(n)

programme.title: The Banker's Guide to the Art Market
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the closed doors of London's art market, revealing how the newly rich of the financial world has pushed prices to unprecedented heights(n)

programme.title: Dambusters Declassified
programme.desc: Actor Martin Shaw takes to the skies in a bid to separate the facts from the myths surrounding the famous 1943 raid by 617 Squadron, which used bouncing bombs to attack German dams. He also talks to the last living RAF veteran of the mission, as well as to a survivor of the flooding caused by the breaching of the Moehne dam, and meets the secret wartime girlfriend of pilot Guy Gibson(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy receives a mysterious letter on Christmas Eve 1925 and realises that the Shelbys are in danger of annihilation, despite his new-found and unprecedented legitimacy. As the enemy closes in, Tommy flees his country house and returns to the only safe place he knows - Small Heath, Birmingham, where the family must find a way to put differences aside, take up arms and fight for survival(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: As the Shelbys come to terms with the shocking events of Christmas Day, Tommy tries to unite his family and makes a decision he may come to regret, enlisting the help of tough Romany Gypsy Aberama Gold, who wants something unusual in return. Jessie Eden confronts Tommy about the workers' pay, warning him that revolution is in the air. As the situation plays out, Tommy's factory manager tells him he has one more meeting, with a mysterious businessman from Paris, but what transpires is no ordinary meeting, with no ordinary businessman(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Italians launch another attack on the Peaky Blinders and Tommy realises that the Shelbys need to evolve if they are to survive, but some of the family are reluctant to part with tradition. As the strike takes hold at the Lanchester factory, Tommy pays a personal visit to Jessie Eden, but is outmanoeuvred when she reveals something she knows about his past, while Changretta plans further ways to continue the vendetta in the most devastating way possible. Elsewhere, Luca makes direct contact with someone at the heart of the Peaky Blinders organisation(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: During the busy summer season a fox family plays below the Carmarthen Fans, lizards bask in the sun on limestone pavements in the upper Swansea valley and hundreds of dragonflies emerge from pools in the uplands near Brecon. Iolo Williams heads to the Black Mountain foothills as sheep are gathered by shepherds on horseback and some dedicated volunteers try to repair a mountain. Ancient botanical cures for ailments and old steam railways are two of many hidden histories(n)

programme.title: England's Forgotten Queen: The Life and Death of Lady Jane Grey
programme.desc: Three days into her reign, the clock is already ticking for Jane as Mary Tudor, determined to seize power of the throne, raises an army. The Duke of Northumberland is sent from the Tower of London to lead Jane's forces against Mary, and heavily armed ships are deployed to prevent her escaping by sea. With both armies matched in size and firepower, the future of the country hangs in the balance(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Jennifer O'Leary examines the impact of Margaret Thatcher's election on the conflict, hearing a shocking admission about the Brighton bomb which almost killed the prime minister and talking to individuals who were involved in smuggling weapons for the IRA. Plus, how Gerry Adams was elected to parliament led to rising tension between the political and paramilitary wings of the Republican movement(n)

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose tells the story of Captain James Cook's historic 1768 voyage to find a vast southern continent. No such land mass was discovered, but the crew of the Endeavour went on to Tahiti and Australia, which Cook claimed for Britain. The ship was almost ripped apart on the Great Barrier Reef, but the captain's navigational skills eventually steered it back home to safety(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between the Citizens and Hornets since the FA Cup final in May, when Pep Guardiola's men ran riot in a thumping 6-0 victory at Wembley Stadium. That was City's 11th successive victory over Watford, a record stretching back to April 2007. Plus, action from Everton v Sheffield United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at the King Power Stadium, Newcastle United v Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park, and Southampton v Bournemouth, which took place on Friday night at St Mary's Stadium(n)

In the opening programme, he stumbles on tawny owls in residence in an Abertillery chimney a thriving colony of rats in the centre of Swansea and a fox family living in an allotment in Cardiff. Iolo also finds the best fed swans in Wales, while a heron visits a Llanberis garden for his daily diet of junk food(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

Armed with just a pestle and mortar, the celebs' first task is to create a curry of their own design using a larder of fresh spices, chilies and herbs and a range of meat, fish and vegetables. programme.title: Peaky Blinders

Drama, starring Cillian Murphy, Sam Claflin, Paul Anderson, Helen McCrory and Cosmo Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets sail through the Kyles of Bute to the Holy Loch and on to Ardentinny, his childhood home overlooking Loch Long. He also discovers a Viking connection in the shape of a vintage sailing boat that takes him to scale the heights of the people's mountain - The Cobbler(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel realises she can use her discovery to take revenge on the Mitchells, so she enlists Lisa's help to get hold of Keanu's phone and set a trap for Sharon. The Taylors gather to celebrate Keegan's birthday, but the celebrations are ruined by tension between Gray and Mitch, while Tiffany's attempts to get Keegan on his own seem fruitless. Louise is less than impressed to see Ben is well and truly back in the Mitchell fold, and Ted and Patrick are nervous about meeting Sheree's mum, Wanda(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Footballer-turned-property expert Dion Dublin, comedians Bob Mortimer and Lucy Porter, and the lovely Debbie McGee join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: EastEnders stalwart Adam Woodyatt, Geordie Shore's Vicky Pattison, former boxing promoter Kellie Maloney, weather presenter Tomasz Schafernaker and DJ and lawyer Judge Jules take part, as the competition hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace continues. Armed with just a pestle and mortar, the celebs' first task is to create a curry of their own design using a larder of fresh spices, chilies and herbs and a range of meat, fish and vegetables. They must then spend time in a professional restaurant kitchen, cooking for paying customers in a busy lunchtime service(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: Family loyalty is vital in rural communities and Kerry's life-long ambition has been to work with her father, Martin. She is overjoyed when he asks her to help him store some wares in a lock-up garage. Meanwhile, a job at the local bowls club gives Kurtan a new sense of purpose(n)

programme.title: FIFA Football Awards 2019
programme.desc: Highlights from the Teatro alla Scala in Milan, Italy, as the finest players and coaches from both the men's and women's games were rewarded for their performances over the last year. Virgil van Dijk, Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo were the nominees for the prestigious Best FIFA Men's Player award claimed by Luca Modric in 2018(n)

programme.title: The Cameron Years
programme.desc: The first in a two-part documentary drawing on an interview with former prime minister David Cameron to explore his leadership, the events that led to the EU referendum and its impact on the political landscape - not to mention his own career. The first film examines how the prime minister went from riding high after the successful 2015 election campaign to defeat in the referendum. Including contributions from Nick Clegg, Iain Duncan Smith, Michael Gove, George Osborne, Ann Widdecombe and Michael Heseltine(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth traverse south-west Wales, where they learn about traditional boats and sheep farming before attending an auction in Carmarthen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the pressure from Mel rising, Sharon suggests going for lunch with Keanu and Louise to talk but is frustrated when Phil invites himself along. Ben tries to impress Jason, one of Phil's business associates, by telling him he can get him a good deal on a fancy car. Ted and Wanda's second date ends in disaster, Rainie loses control after Ian confronts her about her drug-taking and Gray tells Chantelle he's going to see a therapist about his behaviour(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: A tortured Scarlett has to finally face up to her grief. Elsewhere, Lenny comes under pressure to ensure his future freedom is secure, and Dylan seizes the day in a bid to sweep the girl of his dreams off her feet(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Rachel finally comes face to face with Shaun, who offers up the location of the CIA safe house before managing to evade arrest once more. The fugitive soldier flees into an encounter with two mysterious strangers, who offer to provide him with the answers he is looking for. Rachel pays Napier a visit, and the meeting leaves her more convinced than ever that the CCTV network has been compromised(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Evan shows no sign of giving up and pursues Chloe to a cottage in the countryside, where his obsession with her takes a violent turn that leaves her in fear for her life. Cameron receives bad news that forces him to abandon his efforts to help Ric, who has finally been forced to acknowledge his deteriorating health(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Documentary following six junior doctors beginning new placements at Salford Royal hospital. A doctor assigned to the geriatrics ward has to work on his bedside manner, while a colleague in the colorectal ward has her knowledge of bowel surgery put to the test and is given the difficult task of delivering some bad news to a patient(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason presents a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Watford at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between the Citizens and Hornets since the FA Cup final in May, when Pep Guardiola's men ran riot in a thumping 6-0 victory at Wembley Stadium. That was City's 11th successive victory over Watford, a record stretching back to April 2007. Plus, action from Everton v Sheffield United at Goodison Park, Leicester City v Tottenham Hotspur at the King Power Stadium, Newcastle United v Brighton & Hove Albion at St James' Park, and Southampton v Bournemouth, which took place on Friday night at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.desc: Music from the years 1963 to 1968, a period when Terence O'Neill became the new Prime Minister, Ian Paisley emerged as a political figure, and the Civil Rights movement took to the streets demanding reform. programme.desc: With visitor numbers exceeding all expectations, and sales of Harris gin hugely surpassing initial forecasts, the team members work hard to keep up. Domhnall, one of the distillers, happily obliges and takes him out to the site of his grandfather's peat banks. programme.desc: A team of puffer enthusiasts battles to save the rusty, corroded VIC 27 from the scrap heap. On her rusty bow she bears the name Vital Spark, a throwback to her glory days as star of the BBC's 1994 series The Para Handy Tales. The team members look on nervously as she's carefully winched up the purpose built slipway. This is the first time in almost 30 years she's been out of the water, and at last they can assess the condition of her hull prior to ferrying her by canal to Crinan where her restoration begins(n)

programme.desc: England v Tonga (Kick-off 11.15am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' opening Pool C match, which comes from Sapporo Dome. Eddie Jones' men recorded some impressive results in warm-up matches against Wales and Ireland, and will be expecting nothing less than a comfortable victory against a Tongan side that lost 92-7 to New Zealand earlier this month. However, the World Cup has sprung up a number of shock results over the year, and England will be eager to avoid complacency with tougher fixtures on paper to follow against France and Argentina. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, reports from David Flatman, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul heads to the mental health unit, where he joins 10-year-old Tourette's sufferer Oscar at a group therapy session where the youngster hopes to buuild up his confidence. The presenter also meets 13-year-old Lola, who has been struck down by a rare muscle-weakening auto-immune disease that affects just one in a million people, and 15-year old Joe, who has arrived for major surgery to repair a faulty heart valve(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a special version of the talent show, in which stars from the past 13 series of Britain's Got Talent compete against winners from versions of the programme from around the world to impress the viewers and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams. Each week, two acts will make it through to the grand finale. The first is a Golden Buzzer act chosen by one of the judges, or Ant and Dec, while the second is voted through by superfans in the arena audience(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ben, Pat, Vicki and Yvonne pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites music legend Tito Jackson, actress Faye Brookes (Corrie's Kate Connor) and Good Morning Britain weather presenter Laura Tobin to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity(n)

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: A married couple and two sisters take part in the game show, competing to answer visual questions by placing rings on an LED floor in five increasingly difficult stages, with a potential jackpot of more than £50,000 up for grabs. Hosted by Phillip Schofield(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Tensions between Tom and his labourers erupt during the Sanditon cricket match. Charlotte and Sidney continue to clash, resulting in Miss Lambe being placed in terrible danger, while Lord Babington offers Esther a glimpse of a better future. Period drama, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Kris Marshall, Crystal Clarke and Charlotte Spencer(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Great American Trail
programme.desc: Billy's final leg begins in Virginia on the trail of bootleggers dealing in moonshine before he hears the story of the amazing woman who led hundreds of slaves to their freedom. He stops off at a farm where he encounters bizarre heads of all the US presidents, learns about the opioid crisis - the biggest epidemic in American history - and visits the backroom of flamboyant designer Manuel Cuuevas, who was responsible for dressing rock stars including Elvis, Cher and the Beatles. The comedian's tour finally ends in Nashville at the home of country music, the Grand Ole Opry(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas present action from the latest pool fixtures in Japan, featuring Ireland v Scotland, England v Tonga and Italy v Namibia. With commentary from Nick Mullins, Miles Harrison, Simon Ward, Ugo Monye, Ben Kay, Alan Quinlan, Scott Hastings and Danielle Waterman(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Steve Fulcher takes chief suspect Christopher Halliwell to a remote location in the Wiltshire countryside to question him about the whereabouts of missing Swindon girl, Sian O'Callaghan. When Fulcher manages to gain Halliwell's trust, events take a turn that will change the lives of both men for ever, as Halliwell confesses to killing Sian along with a prostitute he murdered and buried in a field many years earlier. Drama, starring Martin Freeman, Joe Absolom and Siobhan Finneran(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. programme.desc: Jamie blames himself for an accident, Graham demands answers and Faith rejects Bear's advances(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary makes Ryan regret not settling his debt and Max stirs up trouble for Audrey in the salon, while Michael reveals to James that he overheard his conversation with Bethany and tackles him about his sexuality(n)

programme.title: Britain's Busiest Motorway
programme.desc: Traffic officers Ray and Brian are called to a collision between a truck and car. The car driver is unhurt, but the truck driver is not happy. Meanwhile, fellow officer Simon and team manager Kevin find some illegally parked truck drivers on the side of the M25, and Kevin finds it hard to make himself understood(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: James opens up to Michael about his secret gay life with his friends in London and Gary uses Ryan's injuries to send a warning to Derek, making it clear that the same will happen to him if he doesn't get far away from Izzy(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: The remains of two young murdered women have been recovered from the Wiltshire countryside due to the actions of Detective Superintendent Steve Fulcher. However, the families of Sian O'Callaghan and Becky Godden-Edwards soon discover that, despite the confession of Christopher Halliwell, getting justice for their loved ones will not be straightforward. Fact-based crime drama, starring Martin Freeman, Imelda Staunton, Siobhan Finneran and Joe Absolom(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: In the final episode, Roz finally decides to confess to Lisa about what happened between her and Wayne, and together they decide to report it to the police. Meanwhile, Kate is rushed into hospital after Fergus finds her unconscious in the boathouse after a night of heavy drinking. When she is discharged from hospital, she convinces Guy to stay for Fergus' birthday party. Lisa becomes increasingly suspicious of Kate's role in Fergus' health, and Kate and Guy finally come to terms with the damage they have done to their family(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: Wales v Georgia. Jill Douglas present action from the Pool D fixture at City of Toyota Stadium, where a much-fancied Wales side were looking to get their campaign off to a perfect start. With commentary from Martin Gillingham, Shane Williams and Jamie Roberts(n)

programme.desc: Alan and Rachel Houston-Holland visit Avington Park in Hampshire, where they invite teams of enthusiasts to compete to find the most rare and valuable items. programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh marks the 75th anniversary of D-Day by creating an accessible garden in Derbyshire for Jack Parrott, a veteran approaching his 100th birthday. The team seeks out advice on clever new ways to create raised beds and patio-size greenhouses to find ways of allowing Jack to once again indulge in his life-long love of gardening(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell take people on an emotional journey reuniting them with long-lost family members. Ann Munro, 63, from Ringwood in Hampshire, gave up her son for adoption when she was 21 and her life ever since has been overshadowed by guilt and the longing to ask for his forgiveness. Louise Kendall, a 28-year-old nurse, lives in Bradford with her four-year-old son. She's hoping to meet her French father for the first time as she travels to Dijon, the city where her parents fell in love(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, passenger experience manager Demi deals with a medical emergency. Meanwhile, border force officer Rob returns to cross-examine unsuspecting passengers arriving into the UK(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: While Cole gets into the holiday spirit for his first Christmas with Maya, Murtaugh hopes he can convince RJ to come back home from Costa Rica for the holidays. At the precinct, Cole and Murtaugh are saddled with a complicated burglary and home invasion case. Meanwhile, Avery's holidays are a bit blue, and Bailey is still trying to figure out her new partner. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop plans to build a swimming pool in the shape of Ma for her birthday surprise, while Charley and Mariette take a well-earned break in France. Starring David Jason and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

Meanwhile, Merton devises a solution to Ventress's retirement worries. Rosemary Leach guest stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield police are puzzled when it seems a face from the past has become the unlikely victim of vandalism, and suspicions are soon aroused over the reasons for his sudden return to the village. Meanwhile, Vernon encounters dangerous wildlife as he stashes his capital in Ashfordly woods, but decides to turn the incident into another money-making scheme. Geoffrey Hughes stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Garland asks Emma to take over running the celebrations for the 50th anniversary of the hotel, while Betsey catches the eyes of a handsome man in the bar - who is most insistent they go for a drink. Meanwhile, Adil tries to break ties with Lucian after giving him more information gleaned from Toby's briefcase and the situation takes a serious turn when MI5 officers arrive at the Halcyon with a warrant to enter and search the premises - prompting Freddie to return to deal with the ensuing crisis(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: The hotel celebrates its 50 year anniversary, but Lucian struggles to keep things in order as pressure mounts and Adil attempts to corner Toby and bring him back from the edge of doing something terrible. Garland and Lady Hamilton find a cause to unite over, while Emma is still confused about the men in her life and finally comes to a decision after talking to Betsey about love during wartime. The tension between Joe and Freddie is palpable: will it spill over? Olivia Williams and Steven Mackintosh star(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun
programme.desc: Carter and Carnarvon's friendship seems permanently shattered, as neither man is willing to back down, while angry mobs take to the street in protest against the Pharaoh's mummy being removed from its final resting place. When Carnarvon falls terminally ill from blood poisoning, Carter swallows his pride and accepts his former patron's peace offering. As stories of a curse dominate the press, Carter reflects on what is to become of the boy-king's remains(n)

programme.title: The Mafia with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster embarks on an eye-opening journey inside the world of the American Mafia, talking to people who have experienced first-hand the money, glamour and violence of the US's most famous organised crime network. In the first of two programmes, Trevor arrives in New York, where he meets John Alite, a former multimillionaire drug dealer who was also one of the most ruthless Mob killers in modern times. He also talks to Michael 'Mikey Scars' DiLeonardo, who became an FBI informant as he faced life in prison(n)
