programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective now has a murder inquiry on his hands as the body found on the moors is identified as 11-year-old Andre Petri. With Kyle Heath still missing, Banks divides his team to search for a connection between the cases, and when post-mortem examinations show Andre was killed by a heroin overdose, it appears the two boys are linked by the illegal drug trade. Starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Johnny Lomax's proposal to Matron causes Nigel Harper to worry about the effect her decision will have on the hospital, and Jill comes into conflict with a strict Catholic family. Ormerod and Frankie face a tough rescue operation as they try to save an injured fisherman trapped on board a sinking ship, while Staff Nurse Taylor's plans for the future are thwarted once again(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The twins abscond from Brockhurst College, but Pop manages to bring them back safe and sound - then lends his services to ensure the boarding school does not have to close. Meanwhile, Primrose makes an unusual request concerning affairs of the heart and Charley launches a bid to buy a local brewery. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop plans to build a swimming pool in the shape of Ma for her birthday surprise, while Charley and Mariette take a well-earned break in France. Starring David Jason and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious assault leads the police to foil an attempt to steal antiques from Ashfordly Hall, and Vernon goes all-out to persuade a wealthy woman to make a charitable contribution toward his donkey sanctuary - but the discovery that there is already one in the area forces him to change his plans. Meanwhile, Merton devises a solution to Ventress's retirement worries. Rosemary Leach guest stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield police are puzzled when it seems a face from the past has become the unlikely victim of vandalism, and suspicions are soon aroused over the reasons for his sudden return to the village. Meanwhile, Vernon encounters dangerous wildlife as he stashes his capital in Ashfordly woods, but decides to turn the incident into another money-making scheme. Geoffrey Hughes stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Garland asks Emma to take over running the celebrations for the 50th anniversary of the hotel, while Betsey catches the eyes of a handsome man in the bar - who is most insistent they go for a drink. Meanwhile, Adil tries to break ties with Lucian after giving him more information gleaned from Toby's briefcase and the situation takes a serious turn when MI5 officers arrive at the Halcyon with a warrant to enter and search the premises - prompting Freddie to return to deal with the ensuing crisis(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: The hotel celebrates its 50 year anniversary, but Lucian struggles to keep things in order as pressure mounts and Adil attempts to corner Toby and bring him back from the edge of doing something terrible. Garland and Lady Hamilton find a cause to unite over, while Emma is still confused about the men in her life and finally comes to a decision after talking to Betsey about love during wartime. The tension between Joe and Freddie is palpable: will it spill over? Olivia Williams and Steven Mackintosh star(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun
programme.desc: Carter and Carnarvon's friendship seems permanently shattered, as neither man is willing to back down, while angry mobs take to the street in protest against the Pharaoh's mummy being removed from its final resting place. When Carnarvon falls terminally ill from blood poisoning, Carter swallows his pride and accepts his former patron's peace offering. As stories of a curse dominate the press, Carter reflects on what is to become of the boy-king's remains(n)

programme.title: The Mafia with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster embarks on an eye-opening journey inside the world of the American Mafia, talking to people who have experienced first-hand the money, glamour and violence of the US's most famous organised crime network. In the first of two programmes, Trevor arrives in New York, where he meets John Alite, a former multimillionaire drug dealer who was also one of the most ruthless Mob killers in modern times. He also talks to Michael 'Mikey Scars' DiLeonardo, who became an FBI informant as he faced life in prison(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim attempts to contact Frank, Zoe urges him to speak to her, and then finds Frank destroying Kim's possessions. Carol is touched when Lorraine asks to come home(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe asks Frank to stop drinking, and when Kim confronts him at the stables, he has some horrifying news for her. Meanwhile, Lorraine moves back in with her mum(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric's car gets repossessed, Rachel is asked to look after Robert and is aghast when he falls in the river. Joe tries to encourage Kim to go to the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tells Chris she cannot wait to leave Home Farm stables, and Frank, who looks terrible after a night of drinking, gets angry with Zoe when she hides his whisky(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Carol tries to make her peace with Lorraine, Zoe and Chris worry about Frank's drinking, and Kim and Neil plan to snatch Dark Star from the stables(n)

programme.desc: Sarah is pleased that Jack seems to be back to his old self, Kathy tells Zoe that she wants to take Dark Star to Kim, and Elizabeth is upset with Eric about his debt(n)

programme.desc: One of Stan's colleagues is about to marry the Inspector's niece - but first he has to survive the stag night. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally wants to go on holiday, but Kevin knows he will not be able to get time off work. Ken starts giving Raquel French lessons, and Mike tells Jim he can start at MVB(n)

programme.desc: Jim starts at the garage, determined to make it work. Liz is embarrassed as Steve is openly rude to Colin, and Bet tackles her over the rumours about her and Colin(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local landowner Les Parnaby loses patience with ramblers wandering across his land and resolves to take matters into his own hands - regardless of the cost. Meanwhile, Ventress's car accident has a strange effect on Vernon Scripps, much to the delight of his many creditors. Rural police drama, starring Lloyd McGuire, Geoffrey Hughes and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica checks into an Atlanta hotel to judge a writing contest, but a room mix-up means that instead of having short stories delivered to her door, she receives a parcel of surveillance photos. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A woman arrives at the depot to claim her lost property, but Stan and Jack have already eaten it. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan gets a new uniform and is proud to wear it - until he sits on a freshly painted chair. Classic comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby splashes out on a new sports car in the hope of impressing Miss Jones, but a cruel practical joke by Philip convinces him he has run over his feline friend Vienna. Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Roger returns to Liverpool, while Pop discovers Edith's new boyfriend George is a con-artist defrauding the ministry - and resolves to teach him a lesson. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser(n)

programme.desc: Nick is worried about leaving Alice while he is on holiday and tells Frank to stop taking things out on him and Kathy. Zoe tells Archie that she could not manage without him(n)

programme.desc: Lorraine tells Carol that she doesn't want her at her party. Jack finds out the hole is due to a collapsed mine. Zoe can't wait to leave her dad behind(n)

programme.desc: Andy tells Jim there's nothing going on between Liz and Colin. Ken tries to teach Raquel how to go shopping in France. Mike suggests that he pays for Mark to attend private school(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Called to the home of an elderly couple who haven't been seen for a while, Janet discovers Eunice Bevan's severed head at the bottom of the stairs, her body at the top, and her husband Joe in bed, emaciated and barely able to speak. Meanwhile, the detective puts her house on the market, admitting her relationship with Ade is over, and three months in to Rachel's marriage to traffic cop Sean, she's starting to feel a little claustrophobic. George Costigan and Nicola Walker guest star, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: In a retrospective episode set immediately after the events of series two, Rachel faces further accusations of masterminding the fatal attack on ex-lover Nick and finds comfort in the arms of ever-faithful Sean, agreeing to marry him. Amid the wedding celebrations, Janet and Ade admit defeat and decide to split up for good, while Gill is duty bound to take action when she catches Pete in a compromising position. The detectives also investigate the case of a businessman suspected of murdering a homeless man(n)

programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates shock reports that changing sea temperatures mean squid and chips could one day replace fish and chips as our national dish, and heads to Thailand, where squid is a national obsession. Kate Quilton goes Down Under to find out about a surprising new meat coming into our supermarkets and Matt Tebbutt heads to Italy to find out how supermarkets can sell pesto at an affordable price when the traditional ingredients are famously expensive(n)

programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: The documentary goes inside the walls of Winchester Prison - which was placed into special measures in December 2017 - at a time when overcrowding, violence and drug use across the prison system in the United Kingdom is at an all-time high. Filmed over a 48-hour period - with access to the prisoners, staff and senior management - the episode focuses on a time of significant unrest, when deteriorating conditions and inmates being locked away for more than 23 hours a day lead to major protests(n)

programme.title: Stath Lets Flats
programme.desc: Stath, Michael & Eagle are nominated for a local letting agents award, thanks to Julian's improvements. programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with captain Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson taking on guest captain Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Manager Fred Sirieix opens the doors of the Italian hotel once more as another group of UK singles check in, hoping to find romance under the Mediterranean sun. Among them is 83-year-old former jazz singer Dorit, who is looking for a 'grey-haired stud'. Is 82-year-old poetry lover John the one for her? Also paired up are 27-year-old accountant Rev and 24-year-old artist Maisie, who has a big story to tell. Plus, there's an update on philosophy graduate Finn and primary school teacher Georgia, who fell for each other last week - despite him being matched up with Jana(n)

programme.title: Treasure Island with Bear Grylls
programme.desc: After two weeks, 11 islanders remain stranded 5,000 miles from home, barely subsisting on a daily diet of coconuts and winkles, as hunger starts to bite. The camp are still completely unaware that Marco and Ivar have stashed away £35,000 and that Emily and Elissa have £10,000 in their backpacks, but suspicions in the group start to rise. Morag decides to take matters into her own hands and sets off a chain of events that threatens to tear the islanders apart(n)

programme.title: The Rebel Chef: My Restaurant Revolution
programme.desc: Chef Gary Usher was discarded as a teenage loser but has turned his life around to make good food at a decent price. Now he sets out to bring his food to Prescot, Merseyside, which has not had a new restaurant in more than 30 years. His plan is to open a bistro in the rundown town centre. But to achieve his dream, he must crowdfund £50,000, build a French-style eaterie out of rubble and convince the sceptical townsfolk to fall in love with the exotic food he will be serving(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: North London couple Jeremy Till and Sarah Wigglesworth draw up plans for an unusual combined home and office made of straw bales and sandbags. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Saleswoman Michelle Evans tries to bring a taste of Miami's South Beach to England's south coast, and golf professional Ben Stimson believes a simple but well-prepared menu will secure him the cash prize. programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go to Spain
programme.desc: Anne Diamond reunites with her old breakfast telly presenting partner Nick Owen, and together with chef Ainsley Harriott, politician's wife Christine Hamilton and actress Vicki Michelle travel to Spain to sample life on the Costa Blanca, following in the footsteps of over five million Brits who travel to the UK's favourite holiday spot every year. Along the way, the five celebrities visit Benidorm, Valencia and Madrid, sampling local culture in the form of wine-making, cuisine and flamenco lessons. They also immerse themselves in Spanish history on a visit to find the Holy Grail(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go to Spain
programme.desc: Anne Diamond, Nick Owen, Ainsley Harriott, Christine Hamilton and Vicki Michelle visit Benidorm, a mecca for thousands of sun-worshipping British tourists each year. The famous five unravel their towels at the beach before taking to the surf by pedalo - and Ainsley and Vicki try their hands at mini golf. After his first taste of traditional Spanish food, self-confessed fussy eater Nick's only desire is a fry-up, though Ainsley takes him well and truly out of his comfort zone as he prepares, cooks and eats a traditional local delicacy - octopus(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go to Spain
programme.desc: After visiting one of Spain's top flamenco venues, Anne Diamond, Nick Owen, Ainsley Harriott, Vicki Michelle and Christine Hamilton buckle up their dancing shoes to perform. The quintet then head to Madrid, where they bask in the splendour of the royal palace which, with more than 3,000 rooms, is one of the largest royal buildings in Europe. On the way back to the Costa Blanca, Nick gets saddle sores as he discovers his inner cowboy on a horse ride through the mountains with Vicki, while Anne, Ainsley and Christine meet some ex-pats at a bowling club in Benitachell(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go to Spain
programme.desc: Anne Diamond, Nick Owen, Ainsley Harriott, Vicki Michelle and Christine Hamilton spend the final days of their Spanish adventure helping out a family-run vineyard on the last day of the grape harvest. Christine and Anne have a tough task of picking the grapes in the sweltering heat, while Ainsley, Vicki and Nick get down and dirty and crush the grapes the old fashioned way - with their feet. Inspired by Spanish cuisine, Ainsley wants their last meal to be special, so enlists the help of local chef Carlos Medina to prepare an authentic feast for his housemates(n)

programme.title: Make You Laugh Out Loud

programme.title: It's Your Fault I'm Fat

programme.title: The World's Biggest Hips
programme.desc: Documentary about four plus-size women who refuse to bow to the pressures of Hollywood size-zero 'ideal', and are proud of their figures. Mikel has hips that are eight feet in circumference and loves her 'unique physique', while Austrian-born Claudia works as a professional model in sunny Los Angeles. Elsewhere, New Yorker Marlena struts her stuff in a dance studio, while Denise makes a living by selling clips of her curves to men who are attracted to the fuller figure(n)

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: Comedian Susan Calman takes a light-hearted jaunt around her homeland to uncover the secrets behind some of Scotland's most famous locations. She begins by visiting Edinburgh and the east coast, where she learns all about the firing of the one o'clock gun at the city's iconic castle, as well as the hiding place of Scotland's crown jewels. The fascinating stories continue below ground, as Susan delves into the ghostly history of Edinburgh's criminal past. Then, in lighter mode, she heads to St Andrews to try out a charming variation of the game of golf(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Julian sets up a blood bank for baby alpacas, but collecting donations is harder than expected. Peter is woken at 2am by an emergency call-out for a pregnant cow, and must be at his sharpest to deliver the calf safely. A sheepdog undergoes treatment to remove a tumour, a cat is in need of castration, and Katie visits a bad-tempered pig with very sore eyes(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes guide to the Mr Kipling Factory in Carlton, Yorkshire, which first opened in the 1970s and was the largest bakery in Europe at the time. Members of the workforce provide an insight into the cutting edge technology that keeps the cakes coming 24 hours a day, while food historian Dr Polly Russell presents a guide to the company's history, revealing how recipes that date back to the 1800s remain popular favourites to this day(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the confectionery company, telling the story of a business that started out making cocoa and drinking chocolate almost 200 years ago, eventually evolving into one of the most famous chocolate brands in the world. But over the past 150 years the company has been rocked by scandal, including a salmonella scare and a hostile takeover bid. This documentary looks at the good and the bad times for Cadbury(n)

programme.title: The Boy Who Can't Stay Awake & Other Medical Mysteries
programme.desc: Documentary featuring Natasha, a young woman who is allergic to almost everything. Cameras also follow Carew, a teenage footballer who falls asleep for days, and Seanin, a 23-year-old student forced to live life like a 90-year-old because of mysterious seizures(n)

Fantasy adventure, with Kristen Stewart, Chris Hemsworth and Charlize Theron(n)

Sci-fi thriller based on the 2000AD comic-strip, starring Karl Urban, Lena Headey and Olivia Thirlby(n)

Comedy sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg and Amanda Seyfried, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane(n)

A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful Country (1959)

Western, starring Robert Mitchum, Julie London, Pedro Armendariz and Jack Oakie(n)

programme.title: The Colditz Story (1954)

Although breaking out is supposedly impossible, the determined inmates refuse to be beaten and continue their bid for freedom. Second World War drama, starring John Mills and Eric Portman(n)

programme.title: Night Passage (1957)

Western, starring James Stewart, Audie Murphy, Dan Duryea and Brandon de Wilde(n)

programme.title: Fantastic Four (2015)

Superhero adventure, starring Miles Teller, Michael B Jordan, Kate Mara and Jamie Bell(n)

James Cameron's action comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Jamie Lee Curtis and Tom Arnold(n)

As his investigations are disrupted at every turn, the detective is horrified to see the population have embraced an ancient pagan lifestyle, and begins to fear they may intend to sacrifice the child in an occult ritual. Director's cut of the horror, starring Edward Woodward, Christopher Lee and Britt Ekland(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Singing teacher Petrina hosts the fourth dinner party in north Surrey, and she believes her experience of entertaining on cruise ships will give her the edge as she plans a showstopper of an evening. However, the diners seem more interested in their host's continued flirting with fellow guest Rob. Will tonight be the night, or will he shy away when he discovers Petrina has sung in the West End and worked with dozens of household names?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Giovanni hosts the final night of the competition in north Surrey, intent on flying the flag for Sicily with some family inspired recipes. Even with help from his father, a tagliatelle tragedy leaves him with Parmesan on his face. Becky is less than impressed with Giovanni's dishes, and matters go from bad to worse when he attempts to serve his home-made pasta. However, poor food doesn't deter a romantic Rob from popping the question to Petrina, sending shockwaves across the dining room. In a final attempt to bag the £1,000, Giovanni invites an opera singer to liven up the proceedings, but his musical initiative may prove too little to save the day(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The host helps Leicester couple James and Drew restore their period house, reclaiming its classic Georgian dimensions and features. In Brixton, south London, a Victorian living room has had all its period features ripped out and replaced with 1970s kitsch and fake Tudor. Can George turn this travesty into a smart and sophisticated family sitting room? Plus, he visits a 300-year-old silk weaver's house in Spitalfields, London, that is perfectly adapted for stylish modern living(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant rhino Asha goes into labour overnight, meaning there is a surprise waiting for the keepers when they arrive to work the next morning. Meanwhile, Malayan tapirs Margery and Betong say goodbye to their daughter, Solo, who is leaving to start a new life at a zoo in Germany, and female dead leaf mantis Minnie is looking for her first partner(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a Chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Professional deep-sea diver Adrian Corrigall and his wife Megan plan to build their new family home in rural East Sussex almost entirely out of concrete, with construction involving cutting-edge technologies conceived in Switzerland and never used to build a house before. However, the perils of being a pioneer soon become evident, and with both schedule and budget under strain, Adrian is forced to resume work as a diver, taking him away from the project for a month at a time(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel return to continue their renovation adventures in France. Two-and-a-half years have passed since the couple swapped their flat in Southend for a 19th-century chateau in France costing only £280,000, and in that time, the pair have worked to transform the derelict property into a fairytale castle. However, around half of the 45 rooms are yet to receive adequate attention, the couple's fledgling events business demands attention, and the outbuildings are still in need of conversion(n)

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: George visits Lucas and Hayley, whose bland and boxy 1970s four-bedroom home sticks out like a sore thumb among the period properties of Saffron Walden, Essex. To make matters worse, some 1980s interior additions have added insult to injury - and the double garage at the side is next to useless. Enter architect Chris Dyson to bring the property bang up to date. But will Lucas and Hayley be happy with his radical plans?(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Railway
programme.desc: On the banks of the River Dee near to Balmoral Castle, volunteers are bringing steam trains back to Royal Deeside, while Edinburgh Pride comes to Waverley station(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary follows the work of the stroke team at south London's King's College Hospital. Laura's family faces a big decision when the 72-year-old grandmother is found to have a blood clot on her brain, while father-of-three Nic, the over-40s National BMX Champion, collapses in reception after suffering an accident while training for a stunt. The medics also treat 83-year-old Marjorie, who is complaining of a pain behind her eye following a recent bout of shingles(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the popular words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and David Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant rhino Asha goes into labour overnight, meaning there is a surprise waiting for the keepers when they arrive to work the next morning. Meanwhile, Malayan tapirs Margery and Betong say goodbye to their daughter, Solo, who is leaving to start a new life at a zoo in Germany, and female dead leaf mantis Minnie is looking for her first partner(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries of veterinary medicine, and treats a German shepherd puppy that needs surgery on his hips and front leg using groundbreaking cell treatment. The vet must also perform delicate surgery on a Chihuahua suffering from a neck deformity compressing its spinal cord - however, one false move could be fatal. Plus, a rescue dog with deformed front legs is brought into the surgery(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty sets out to learn how kippers are produced, and finds out why this nutritious smoked fish is no longer a breakfast favourite. Kate Quilton is in Germany to investigate gherkins, discovering a space-age machine that can pickle food in seconds. Matt Tebbutt and Helen Lawal try to discover why rice cakes are white when they are made of brown rice(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Michelle and Gary Cole from Birmingham have both suffered from ill health over the past few years and have decided that the rejuvenating effects of the Spanish sun are just what the doctor ordered. They're looking for a holiday home where they and their two kids can spend quality time together. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties on the Costa Blanca for their £65,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Anne Hogarth first fell in love with Lanzarote over a decade ago and now, after several visits to the island since, is looking for a holiday home to enjoy in her retirement. With her daughter Rachel along for moral support, Jonnie Irwin shows Anne five properties in the resort of Puerto del Carmen for her £85,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Matt and Lucia's house in Royal Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire, is bulging at the seams, as it is home to not only them but three grown-up children from previous relationships. Matt loves it but Lucia does not, so while Kirstie Allsopp tries to change the layout, Phil Spencer finds some alternative properties. But will the family choose to up sticks or stay where they are?(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim races to get his chateau ready for the first paying guests, battling to get a fence built for a marauding flock of 25 sheep, while partner Krys tries to balance the books(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cosmetic surgery fan Shanice hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in and around Guildford, Surrey, where risky menu choices and a fellow diner's laugh become talking points(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired estate agent Vivi hosts the second dinner party in and around Guildford, and hopes to win with a Norwegian feast, as tensions rise between Shanice and Charlotte(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sales manager Charlotte hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in and around Guildford, hoping to impress her guests enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, she has taken a huge risk with an Asian-inspired menu, which could prove to be too exotic for entertainments manager David(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The penultimate day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sees children's entertainments manager David playing host. Having worked in the entertainments industry all his life, he is no stranger to showing people a good time, and employs his colleagues to put on a performance, while keeping his food simple. Is he forgetting the key ingredients of a dinner party?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sales director Neil devises a sophisticated menu, dismissing his predecessors' efforts as unambitious. However, his unusual fayre is tempered by his choice of yodelling entertainment, which threatens to hinder his chance of scooping the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In this art deco special, George takes on a challenging period property in Newquay, owned by Gareth and Frances Jenkins. The building was once an architectural gem, but over the years has been the victim of unsympathetic modernisations, making it unrecognisable from its 1930s heyday. The challenge is to strip away the ugly accretions to reveal the house in all its glory - while also making it suitable for modern family living. Plus, George visits a stunning seaside home and Britain's biggest collection of neon signs(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Young meerkat couple Beagle and Huskie become parents for the first time, and spider monkey Kiara is finding it increasingly difficult to bring up her first daughter, Laura. Meanwhile, vets decide to examine painted dog K'mana's ankle because of an old injury, leaving her partner Ville alone to cope with their seven adolescent puppies(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick performs a risky procedure to save the life of an 18-week-old cockapoo puppy that has fractured its neck following an accident in the garden. He also comes to the aid of Romanian stray Geoffrey, who was found abandoned and emaciated before being adopted by Kent couple John and Stacey, and an accident-prone cat called MK who has fallen from a window(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon to meet Kevin McCabe, a man who wanted to build one of the biggest houses the programme has ever seen. Finally, seven years after it began, the herculean task of building this giant cob castle is complete. Has it proven to be the great home he originally intended, and does it prove as he hoped that cob is a viable material for contemporary architecture?(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick gets to work on the walled garden in the spring and buys a mini tractor for the job - but it may not fit through the gate. Angel designs another suite, with black walls and mosaic mirrors, and decorates the entire dining room with blossoms so a guest can make a special wedding proposal. The couple also consider the possibility of truffle farming(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients whose visions of the world are rocked by life-changing events, including 44-year-old Sergio, who is flown in by air ambulance after a cycling accident. Another cyclist - 71-year-old Alan - arrives in resus after being in a collision with a van. Doctors suspect a head injury and multiple fractures. Alan's wife Jenny reveals how she met him and how a sudden accident involving her son burst their idyll and took their lives on an unexpected path(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: A woman who has been badly cut in a Saturday night pub fight endures an agonising wait for medical care, while medics attend a habitual caller who has requested help rather than use medication to control his symptoms - one of many non-urgent cases handled by the Wiltshire's(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics help a drunk driver who has crashed on a dual carriageway, but find themselves on the receiving end of a vicious attack. In Bromsgrove, an ambulance comes under siege(n)

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May travel to America for an interstate road trip, completing various challenges along the way. programme.title: Top Gear

The glamorous cars are then put through their paces in a gruelling six-hour endurance challenge at Silverstone. Plus, Countryfile's Matt Baker drops in to the studio(n)

programme.title: Hurricane Man
programme.desc: Josh and his documentary team are in the wake of Typhoon Cimaron in Japan, as Hurricane Lane explodes onto the scene, barrelling towards Hawaii and threatening historic landfall(n)

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A driver is stopped attempting to smuggle 15kg of cocaine across the border, and a truck driver's modified cab attracts attention at the World Trade Bridge(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of the Staffordshire Police force. Rookie cop Ash is in the thick of it during a late night brawl in Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist and explorer leads a team on a mission to summit a remote and unclimbed mountain in the Stauning Alps of Greenland, crossing a vast river and skiing across a glacier(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Gavin starts his job in Cardiff and, while Stacey is thrilled to be back in Wales, Smithy has to come to terms with living in Essex without his best friend. Nessa has taken up residence in Dave's caravan in Sully and is getting ready for Neil's christening. The event means a big reunion for the gang, at which Smithy receives surprising news from Nessa, and Stacey has a special request for her husband(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Dave is unhappy about Nessa's plans to let Smithy see the baby, while Stacey ensures Gavin sticks to his promise as she tries to find a new job. Meanwhile, Dawn and Pete join Pam and Mick for a beer and curry night. Comedy, starring Joanna Page and Mathew Horne(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Bryn is in his element as he awaits the arrival of the Essex boys, who are having a big night out in Cardiff and staying for a sleepover, but Smithy is concerned about seeing Nessa again after their last meeting. Meanwhile, Stacey tells Gavin about a worrying discovery, and Pam and Mick discuss their plans for a new conservatory(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Everyone gathers in Billericay as Pete and Dawn prepare to renew their wedding vows. Gavin feels uncomfortable about Gwen and Bryn knowing the details of his sex life, and Dave hears about the shocking events of the curry night. Meanwhile, Smithy goes beyond the call of friendship when he makes a staggering suggestion(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey

programme.desc: Yvonne and Fernando frantically try to get their Lake District hotel ready for a big tour group, while shopping and heavy lifting are on the cards for Steph and Dom in Scotland(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

Once the landlord agrees to give them access, they discover terrible conditions and a back yard piled with rubbish(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Following a tip-off, Harrow Enforcement officers are shocked to discover an industrial-scale cannabis farm in a house. After complaints of all-night parties in a quiet street in Darwen, Lancashire, letting agent Paul pays the revellers a visit. The house is in terrible shape, but the young tenants promise they will clear it all up and keep the noise down. Instead, however, the partying gets worse, with some of the tenants' 'associates' making nasty threats(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given birth to species and sub-species that exist nowhere else in the world. He begins by revealing how Galapagos rose from the ocean four million years ago(n)

programme.title: Fit to Rule: How Royal Illness Changed History
programme.desc: Historic Royal Palaces chief curator Lucy Worsley explores how the physical and mental health of Britain's monarchs has shaped the history of the nation, examining medical problems, doctors' reports, personal correspondence and possessions to gain an insight into the real men and women behind the royal portraits. She begins by examining the Tudor and Stuart dynasties, including the difficulty Henry VIII experienced producing a male heir, the cause of his daughter Mary I's phantom pregnancy and the special orthopaedic boots worn by Charles I(n)

programme.title: Winston Churchill: Winning the War, Losing the Peace
programme.desc: A look at Winston Churchill's battle to be elected Prime Minister just weeks after VE Day. The war leader was confident of victory, but ended up being humiliated at the polls with the Conservative party almost annihilated. Surprising revelations from first-hand witnesses, including Sir Max Hastings, Juliet Gardiner, Anthony Beevor and Dave Douglas, help to uncover whether Churchill's rejection was a mark of ingratitude, or the most mature decision ever made by a democracy(n)

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes(n)

programme.title: Three Steps in the Dark (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy eccentric invites all the members of his family to his country house, so he can discuss the contents of his Will. However, when he is suddenly killed, everyone present is a suspect. Murder mystery, starring Greta Gynt and Hugh Sinclair(n)

programme.title: Looking for Victoria
programme.desc: Prunella Scales, who has played Queen Victoria on stage for two decades, takes on the role for TV to look back at the fascinating life of the monarch, visiting many of the houses and places in her life. Timothy West, Charles Dance and Andrew Sachs co-star as key courtiers and politicians of her era, using only their actual recorded words. The first of two episodes follows Victoria's life from childhood and marriage to the premature death of her husband(n)

programme.title: The Thirty-Nine Steps (1978)
programme.desc: An innocent man is informed of a devious plan to start the First World War by murdering a foreign minister. Almost immediately, he is framed for murder and embarks on a perilous cross-country trek to prove his innocence, pursued by both the police and ruthless spies. Thriller - the third film adaptation of John Buchan's novel - starring Robert Powell, Karen Dotrice, John Mills and George Baker(n)

programme.title: Dr Crippen (1963)
programme.desc: A doctor stands trial for poisoning his wife and fleeing the country with his lover. Fact-based crime drama, starring Donald Pleasence and Samantha Eggar(n)

programme.title: Lusitania: 18 Minutes That Changed World War One
programme.desc: Docudrama about the sinking of British ocean liner RMS Lusitania 11 miles off the southern coast of Ireland by a German U-boat on May 7, 1915, which resulted in the deaths of 1,198 passengers and crew and was a key factor in the US's entry into the First World War. The events of that day are brought to life using archive footage, photographs, documents, dramatic reconstructions and expert opinion by marine historians, as well as first-hand testimonies by survivors and the submarine captain(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Primary School
programme.desc: This edition of the documentary about life at Gascoigne Primary School in Barking, east London, follows three very different boys the school supports in various ways. Year 5 pupil Cleyson is on the 'gifted and talented' list, but he has a hot temper and has recently been temporarily excluded for hurting another pupil, and staff are keen to help him avoid offending again as it could jeopardise his education. The school assigns teaching assistant Luke Brown to support 11-year-old Lewis, who has ADHD, while seven-year-old Toby is finding settling down in Year 2 a slow process(n)

programme.title: Time Without Pity (1957)
programme.desc: An alcoholic writer returns home after years living abroad when he learns his estranged son has been convicted of murder and sentenced to hang. Mystery, starring Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern and Peter Cushing(n)

programme.title: Filming My Father: In Life and Death
programme.desc: In 2010, Steve Isaac was a fit 51-year-old father of three when he was diagnosed as having motor neurone disease and given two years to live. This film, to which his 22-year-old son Fraser contributed footage, documents the details of Steve's progressive condition and the decline in his health as muscles wasted away, as well as revealing the effect it has had on his family, who have been pushed to the brink by his desire to live at almost any cost. Four-and-a-half years on, Steve has defied medical expectation, but his only method of communicating now is blinking(n)
